name: Rabab
title: Loyal Wife of the Imam
shortDescription: The loyal wife of Imam Hussain (AS) and mother of Hazrat Ali Asghar (AS) who, after Ashura, wept and never sat under a shade for the rest of her life.
fullStory:
Rabab bint Imru' al-Qays was the loyal and beloved wife of Imam Hussain (AS). She is the mother of Hazrat Sakina and Hazrat Ali Asghar (Abdullah al-Rabi'). Rabab was a woman of noble character, deep faith, and from an aristocratic lineage. Imam Hussain (AS) loved her deeply and had expressed his affection for her in poetry.

Rabab was present at the event of Karbala and witnessed the most painful scenes in history with her own eyes. One of the hardest moments for her was when her six-month-old infant, Ali Asghar (AS), was martyred with thirsty lips in the arms of Imam Hussain (AS) by Harmala's three-pronged arrow. Rabab endured this massive grief with Zaynabi patience.

After Ashura, she was taken captive along with the other women, enduring the arduous journey to Kufa and Sham. In Ibn Ziyad's court, when they brought the severed head of Imam Hussain (AS), Rabab embraced her husband's head and recited heart-rending elegies for him.

After returning to Medina, Rabab never forgot the tragedy of Karbala. The elders of Quraysh proposed to her, but she said: 'I will accept no one as a husband after the son of the Messenger of God.'

As a sign of mourning for her husband, Rabab never sat under a shade for the rest of her life. She wept under the scorching sun of Medina until, exactly one year after the event of Ashura, she passed away from intense grief.
quoteArabic: إِنَّ الَّذي كانَ نُوراً يُسْتَضاءُ بِهِ بِكَرْبَلاءَ قَتيلٌ غَيْرُ مَدْفُونِ
quote: Indeed, the light by which guidance was sought lies killed and unburied in Karbala.
